summary:
- publisher=UKMTO, title=Advisories - 2026, directness=high, why=Primary source for 2026 maritime advisories including Strait of Hormuz, next=https://www.ukmto.org/ukmto-products/advisories/2026  
- publisher=UKMTO, title=UKMTO Products, directness=medium, why=Parent page linking to Advisories, Monthly Reports, and Warnings; confirms structure for accessing 2026 advisories, next=https://www.ukmto.org/ukmto-products  
- publisher=UKMTO, title=Monthly Reports, directness=medium, why=Monthly Maritime Security Update Reports may contain forward-looking analysis or early indicators for May 2026 Strait of Hormuz conditions, next=https://www.ukmto.org/ukmto-products/monthly-reports  
- publisher=UKMTO, title=Contact Us, directness=high, why=Provides verified emergency and general contact channels to verify advisories directly with UKMTO Operations Centre, next=https://www.ukmto.org/contact-us  
- publisher=UKMTO, title=About UKMTO, directness=medium, why=Confirms UKMTO’s authority and 25+ year role in verifying maritime security intel for critical routes like Strait of Hormuz, supports credibility of advisories, next=https://www.ukmto.org/about-us  
- publisher=UKMTO, title=Best Management Practices, directness=low, why=Relevant for mitigation protocols but not advisory content; may inform operational response if advisory is issued, next=https://www.ukmto.org/best-management-practices  

Note: No 2026 Strait of Hormuz advisory content is yet published (as of search), but the Advisories - 2026 page is the authoritative location to monitor for May 2026 updates. Contact UKMTO via watchkeepers@ukmto.org or +44 (0) 2392 222060 for real-time verification or pre-release alerts.
